etymology.description: 攪(攪) (jiǎo, "stir") is associated with using hands or tools to mix or disturb substances. 拌(拌) (bàn, "mix") involves combining substances together, often using a tool to achieve an even blend.
etymology.interpretation: 攪(攪) (jiǎo, "stir") involves the action of agitating a substance to disturb it, suggesting motion within. In combination, 拌(拌) (bàn, "mix") indicates the purpose of the agitation, aiming for a uniform distribution of different elements. Thus, 攪拌(攪拌) (jiǎobàn) conveys actively stirring to achieve thorough mixing, integrating the movements of disturbing and combining.

examples.chinese: 他需要搅拌这汤(他需要攪拌這湯)。
examples.english: He needs to stir this soup.

examples.chinese: 请用勺子慢慢搅拌(請用勺子慢慢攪拌)。
examples.english: Please stir slowly with a spoon.

examples.chinese: 在加入鸡蛋后(在加入雞蛋後)，继续搅拌混合物(繼續攪拌混合物)。
examples.pinyin: Zài jiārù jīdàn hòu, jìxù jiǎobàn hùnhéwù.
examples.english: After adding the eggs, continue to stir the mixture.
